Title: Expression of a constitutively active nitrate reductase increases SARS-CoV-2 Spike protein production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves that otherwise show traits of senescence

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nitrogen-rich transport/storage amino acid facilitating nitrogen remobilization from senescent tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: very low in NI leaves; strongly induced at late time points in CoVLP samples
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species; reduced when NbNR1S523D was co-expressed
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: up-regulation correlates with increased asparagine accumulation in CoVLP-producing leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbASN1 belongs to Cluster I ASN genes with senescence-associated expression. 2. It is among the most strongly induced genes in CoVLP samples at 6 DPI. 3. Its induction correlates with increased asparagine accumulation and is reduced by co-expression of NbNR1S523D.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; amino-acid mass spectrometry; RT-qPCR in helper-gene assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ASN is enzymatic, the study mainly uses ASN genes as markers and correlates of nitrogen remobilization; no direct ASN transgenic manipulation was performed.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful marker of senescence-associated nitrogen remobilization to monitor host metabolic status in molecular farming

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: very low in NI leaves; strongly induced at late time points in CoVLP samples
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species; reduced when NbNR1S523D was co-expressed
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: up-regulation correlates with asparagine accumulation in CoVLP leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbASN2 is a senescence-related Cluster I ASN gene strongly induced during CoVLP accumulation. 2. Its expression parallels rising asparagine content and is lowered by NbNR1S523D co-expression.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; amino-acid mass spectrometry; RT-qPCR in helper-gene assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marker and likely contributor to asparagine accumulation, but not directly functionally manipulated.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: marker for nitrogen remobilization state during recombinant protein production

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant assimilatory nitrate reductase; MoCo domain, heme-binding cytochrome b5 domain, FAD/NADPH-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: nitrate-to-nitrite reduction step of primary nitrogen assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces nitrate to nitrite using reducing equivalents from NAD(P)H
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: asparagine; amino acid pool; recombinant SARS-CoV-2 Spike protein
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: asparagine supports nitrogen remobilization; amino acid pool supports protein biosynthesis; increased Spike protein yield supports vaccine production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: NbNR1 transcripts were rapidly depleted at 2-3 DPI after agroinfiltration, with partial recovery in P19 samples at later time points but sustained repression in CoVLP samples
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: down-regulated during CoVLP production in infiltrated leaves; overexpressed from a transgene in helper-protein assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient co-expression of P19 and SARS-CoV-2 Spike/CoVLP; light-dark regulation relevant to NR activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in nitrate reduction; treated as a targetable limiting step for improving nitrogen supply but not formally established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: wild-type NbNR1 overexpression had no significant beneficial effect on Spike protein accumulation compared with empty vector control
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous NbNR1 expression is strongly repressed in CoVLP-producing leaves, consistent with reduced nitrogen reduction during senescence-like responses. 2. NbNR1 was selected as a helper gene to restore nitrate reduction capacity in agroinfiltrated leaves. 3. Co-expression of wild-type NbNR1 did not significantly increase SARS-CoV-2 Spike protein accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; agroinfiltration co-expression; immunoblot; capillary western immunoassay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: ENA PRJEB64341; iTRAQ proteomics reported for related CP analyses in the same study, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: wild-type overexpression showed no yield benefit; constitutive NR manipulation may alter defence/senescence balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the study logic, the beneficial phenotype was specific to the constitutively active mutant rather than the wild-type enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: wild-type gene alone is of limited utility; informs that post-translational deregulation is required for metabolic engineering benefit

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: engineered plant assimilatory nitrate reductase with Ser523Asp substitution in regulatory phosphorylation site; MoCo, heme, FAD domains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: nitrate-to-nitrite reduction step of primary nitrogen assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: engineered nitrate reductase variant predicted to constitutively reduce nitrate to nitrite and resist dark-induced phosphorylation-mediated inactivation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Spike protein is the vaccine antigen for COVID-19 vaccine production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently overexpressed in agroinfiltrated leaves together with CoVLP construct
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient co-expression with P19 and SARS-CoV-2 Spike/CoVLP under light/dark growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: engineered key control step to maintain nitrate reduction capacity across the diel cycle
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increased Spike protein accumulation by ~55% relative to empty-vector control in CoVLP-producing leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A Ser523Asp substitution was introduced into NbNR1 to abolish dark inactivation and create a constitutively active, light-insensitive nitrate reductase. 2. NbNR1S523D accumulated more strongly than wild-type NbNR1 in infiltrated leaves. 3. Co-expression of NbNR1S523D increased SARS-CoV-2 Spike accumulation by ~55%. 4. NbNR1S523D reduced expression of several CoVLP-induced defence and senescence markers, including NbASN1 and NbASN2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed engineering; transient co-expression; western blotting for NR and Spike; standardized capillary western immunoassay; RT-qPCR of defence/senescence markers
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: more intense necrosis and leaf curling accompanied the higher Spike accumulation, indicating aggravated visible stress symptoms
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the principal causal metabolic engineering gene in the paper; benefit is attributed to improved nitrate reduction and partial suppression of senescence/defence-associated reprogramming.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate helper gene for transient expression platforms; constitutively active NR variants may be stacked with other metabolism-support traits to increase recombinant protein yield, but visible stress trade-offs should be monitored

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: senescence-associated metabolic reprogramming and nitrogen remobilization
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: asparagine supports nitrogen remobilization; altered senescence status influences recombinant protein production
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: early-induced in CoVLP samples, peaking at 3-4 DPI
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: putative transcriptional activation of senescence program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: associated with induction of senescence-associated genes in CoVLP leaves
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: early senescence regulator candidate associated with establishment of a metabolic state unfavorable to sustained protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: up-regulation marks onset of senescence-like responses during CoVLP production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbNAC87 is an early NAC transcription factor induced upon S protein expression. 2. Its induction supports the conclusion that CoVLP production activates senescence regulatory networks. 3. The senescence program correlates with nitrogen remobilization and reduced host productivity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper it is a marker/regulatory candidate rather than functionally manipulated core causal gene.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: associated with senescence and likely reduced host metabolic capacity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: possible future target for suppressing senescence in molecular farming, but not functionally validated here

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: asparagine supports nitrogen remobilization; altered senescence status influences recombinant protein production
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: early-induced in CoVLP samples, peaking at 3-4 DPI
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate early driver of senescence-like host responses that may restrict recombinant protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: up-regulated during CoVLP production as part of senescence TF network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbNAC81 is strongly induced early during CoVLP production. 2. Its profile supports rapid activation of senescence regulatory circuitry in infiltrated leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally tested in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: associated with senescence-linked decline in primary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate anti-senescence engineering target for future work

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: asparagine supports nitrogen remobilization; senescence impacts protein yield
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: late-induced and more strongly up-regulated in CoVLP than P19 samples
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: associated with late senescence gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: late senescence regulator candidate associated with worsening metabolic conditions for recombinant protein production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: marks late senescence activation in CoVLP leaves
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbNAC29 is a late NAC induced more strongly by CoVLP production than by P19 alone. 2. It supports sustained senescence signalling during Spike accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as part of the senescence transcriptional network; not directly manipulated.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: associated with progressive decline of productive leaf metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target to delay senescence in biofactory leaves

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: senescence transition; chloroplast decline versus senescence balance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N remobilization; impacts host productivity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: initially down-regulated after agroinfiltration then re-induced in CoVLP samples at later time points
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: associated with late re-induction of senescence program
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate regulator contributing to senescence-associated reprogramming that limits host protein-production capacity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: re-induced with Spike accumulation in CoVLP leaves
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbNAC2 is transiently suppressed by agroinfiltration but later re-induced specifically in CoVLP samples. 2. Its expression tracks Spike accumulation and senescence onset.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role inferred from homology and expression only.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: associated with senescence and reduced chloroplast/primary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for future regulatory engineering

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N remobilization; senescence affects product yield
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: induced at late time points in CoVLP samples
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: associated with activation of senescence-related genes and chlorophyll degradation pathways
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: CoVLP accumulation; ethylene-related signalling inferred in discussion
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: strong senescence regulators whose induction indicates a host state antagonistic to sustained recombinant protein production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: late induction accompanies CoVLP-associated senescence traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Four ORE1/ORS1-like NAC genes are induced late during CoVLP production. 2. Their induction supports activation of a canonical senescence regulatory network in N. benthamiana leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped because the paper treats them together as highly homologous ORE genes rather than individually functionally testing each one.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: senescence activation likely reduces host growth and biosynthetic capacity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential anti-senescence editing targets in transient expression platforms

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: chloroplast development and maintenance of photosynthesis-associated gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: product yield depends on host primary metabolism and photosynthetic competence
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: transiently induced at 2 DPI after agroinfiltration, then progressively down-regulated, more strongly in CoVLP samples
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: photosynthesis-associated genes including LHCBs and chlorophyll biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation (positive regulators of chloroplast-related genes)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: decline in GLK expression correlates with depletion of PAG transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: loss of GLK activity marks a shift away from productive chloroplast metabolism, potentially constraining recombinant protein production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: down-regulation accompanies repression of chloroplast-related genes during CoVLP production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Four GLK genes were identified and profiled in N. benthamiana leaves. 2. They show early transient induction followed by stronger repression in CoVLP samples. 3. Their repression correlates with reduced expression of light-harvesting and chlorophyll-biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulatory importance is inferred from known GLK biology and correlated transcript patterns; not directly manipulated here.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: repression implies compromised photosynthetic performance and host fitness
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: maintaining GLK activity could be explored to sustain chloroplast function in production leaves
